decisions.title: Static proof parity for event-driven refresh is part of Block 6 baseline
decisions.description: After Block 5, the next highest-value gap was not new runtime wiring but stale quality-engine assumptions. Topology-doctor, runtime-bindings, and drift-detect now treat emulator NATS dependency and consumer/emulator refresh durables as first-class invariants before the block moves on to deeper live diagnostics.
alternatives: Leave the new durables visible only to runtime smoke
Skip analyzer updates and rely only on contract-audit plus scenario-smoke

decisions.title: Trace-pack must expose NATS monitor state for event-driven refresh diagnostics
decisions.description: Block 6 phase 3 established that trace-pack should collect NATS monitor health and JetStream state alongside app endpoints. Event-driven refresh failures are no longer diagnosable only through service logs; missing NATS monitor, missing jsz data, or missing validator runtime now show up as explicit evidence items.
alternatives: Keep trace-pack limited to compose status, app endpoints, configs, and logs
Rely only on scenario-smoke output for event-driven refresh failures

decisions.title: Block 6 closes only with dual-path proof through raccoon-cli
decisions.description: Phase 3 closed after proving both operational edges through the same quality engine. With the cluster absent, scenario-smoke and trace-pack now fail fast and expose missing services plus NATS monitor evidence. With the cluster healthy, happy-path and check-deep pass end-to-end, so refresh-oriented diagnostics are no longer only more verbose; they are validated in the canonical raccoon-cli workflow.
alternatives: Stop after readiness-probe and trace-pack improvements without rerunning live cluster proof
Rely on service logs and ad hoc smoke instead of closing through happy-path plus quality-gate deep
